steps.title: Clarify what the hypothesis predicts
steps.content: The hypothesis claims hardening damages waterbird communities more severely than development does. A direct test requires comparing a site dominated by hardening (with minimal development) against a site dominated by development (with minimal hardening). The hardening-heavy site should show lower IWCI scores.

steps.title: Match the prediction to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A delivers this test: Back River emphasizes development with minimal hardening and achieves higher IWCI; Margothy emphasizes hardening with minimal development and achieves lower IWCI. This proves hardening's impact exceeds development's. Choice B contradicts this pattern; choices C and D mix both variables in the same direction, making it impossible to isolate hardening's effect.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
